steps: Perform PCR using OneTaq and with T7 and Sp6 primers using pGEM-T easy with probe inserted as template. Do 5+ reactions to ensure ample PCR product. Annealing temperature 40.5°C, extension time time appropriate for length of probe (typically around 30 seconds). %s %s UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023 Deoxynucleotide Solution Set - 25 umol of each New England Biolabs Catalog #N0446S OneTaq DNA Polymerase - 1,000 units New England Biolabs Catalog #M0480L
Gel extract PCR product and calculate concentration of gel extracted product. QIAquick Gel Extraction Kit Qiagen Catalog #28704

Perform in vitro transcription (3 reactions) with either T7 or Sp6 polymerase depending on anti-sense orientation of probe in pGEM-T Easy. Prepare below reaction and incubate at 37°C for 2 hours. %s %s Reagent Volume (μL) 10x Transcription Buffer 2 10x DIG 2 Protector RNase Inhibitor 0.5 Sp6 or T7 RNA polymerase 1 template (500ng) variable Ultrapure H20 to 20μL Protector RNase Inhibitor Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3335402001 T7 RNA Polymerase Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10881767001 SP6 RNA Polymerase Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10810274001 DIG RNA Labeling Mix Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #11277073910 02:00:00, incubation Undetermined, incubation Use RNase-free reagents for all steps and thoroughly RNase Zap all surfaces and pipettes for this step and all subsequent steps of probe preparation.

Prepare a gel mold and electrophoresis box by spraying thoroughly with RNase Zap followed by washing out with MilliQ water. In addition, RNase Zap and rinse with MilliQ the interior of a bottle and prepare a 2% agarose gel in this RNase-free bottle. RNase Zap Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #R2020-250ML
After 2 hour incubation, proceed to RNeasy RNA cleanup with on column DNase I digestion. RNeasy Mini Kit Qiagen Catalog #74104 RNase-Free DNase Set Qiagen Catalog #79254
Combine all three reactions and volume up to 100μL with Ultrapure H 2 0. UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023
Add 350μL RLT buffer and mix well.
Add 250μL100% ethanol and mix well by pipetting. Proceeded immediately to the next step.
Transfer 700μL to a RNeasy Mini spin column that is in a 2mL collection tube, close lid and centrifuge for 15s at 10,000 rpm (8,000g). Discard flow through.
Add 350μL Buffer RW1, close lid, and centrifuge for 15s at 10,000 rpm.
Combine 10μL DNase I stock solution and 70μL Buffer RDD and mix gently by inverting the tube.
Add 80μL of DNase I/Buffer RDD solution directly to the RNeasy column membrane, close lid, and let it sit on the bench for 15min at room temperature. 00:15:00, incubation
Add 350μL Buffer RW1 to column, close lid, centrifuge for 15s at 10,000 rpm. Discard flow-through.
Add 500μL Buffer RPE to column, close lid, and centrigue for 15s at 10,000 rpm. Discard flow-through.
Add 500μL Buffer RPE to column, close lid, and centrifuge for 2 min at 10,000 rpm. Discard flow-through. 00:02:00, centrifugation
Place RNeasy column into a new 2mL collection tube, close lid, and centrifuge at max speed for 1 min.

Place RNeasy column into a 1.5mL collection tube (supplied with kit) and add 50μL of Ultrapure H 2 0 directly to the column membrane. Close the lid and centrifuge for 1 min at 10,000 rpm. UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023
Remove eluate from collection and re-apply to the column for a second wash.
Remove 2ul of eluate and put into a new 1.5mL tube. Store the remainder of the eluate at -80°C until use.
Add 8μL of Ultrapure H 2 0 and incubate at 60°C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, incubation Undetermined, incubation UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #10977023
Add a clear loading dye (e.g. 40% sucrose) and run on the 2% RNase-free agarose gel to confirm size and quality of product.
Prepare an oven that can accommodate your slide boxes at 65°C. Undetermined, oven
Thaw slides at room temperature (“air-dry”) for 15-20 min. 00:15:00
Rinse slides in 1X DEPC PBS to re-hydrate tissue. This typically requires ~3mL of 1X DEPC PBS

Narcotize animals in 0.1% Tricaine prepared in 40% Holtfreter's. Remove tissue of interest. Deposit directly into 4% PFA prepared in 1X DEPC PBS. All solutions and tools/bottles should be RNase-free, being prepared with DEPC-treated water or being cleaned with RNase Zap, respectively. After survival surgeries always keep animals overnight in 40% Holtfreter's supplemented with 0.5% sulfamerazine. Tricaine methanesulfonate MS222 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)
Fix tissues in 4% PFA prepared in 1X DEPC PBS for 1-2 hr at room temperature with gentle rocking or overnight at 4°C with gentle rocking.
Remove 4% PFA/1X DEPC PBS and wash in 1X DEPC PBS by gently rocking tissue in 1X DEPC PBS for 30 min.

Transfer tissue through a sucrose series, starting with 5% sucrose in 1X DEPC PBS, then 10%, 20%, and eventually 30%. Change to higher percent sucrose when tissue sinks. Once tissue sinks in 30% sucrose in 1X DEPC PBS proceed to the next step. Alternatively, tissue can be rocked overnight in 30% at 4°C.

Add approximately equal volume of OCT compound to the 30% sucrose/1X DEPC PBS. Use a gentle inversion rocker to let it equilibrate for at least 30 minutes. Tissue-Tek® O.C.T. Compound, Sakura® Finetek VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #25608-930
Prepare a dry-ice/ethanol “bath” and RNase Zap forceps. RNase Zap Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #R2020-250ML

Remove tissue from 30% sucrose/OCT solution and move into tissue mold. Fill mold with OCT and orient tissue as desired. Tissue-Tek® O.C.T. Compound, Sakura® Finetek VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #25608-930
After freezing, move tissue into plastic bags and then store at -80°C until sectioning
Place mold with tissue into dry ice/ethanol bath to freeze OCT.

Section tissue on a cryostat. Sections can be 10-20 microns (usually 16 microns). Collect sections on Superfrost Plus slides. Superfrost Plus Microscope Slies Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #4951PLUS4
Air-dry the sections 30 min-2 hrs. Store at -80 o C or continue on to hybridization.
Fix slides in 4% PFA DEPC 1X PBS for 10 min. 00:10:00
Rinse in DEPC PBT
Wash twice in DEPC PBT, 5 min. each.

Treat with Proteinase K in DEPC PBS for 10 min. Do this in a bucket with 200 mL DEPC 1X PBS with 1 μg/mL Proteinase K. 00:10:00 Proteinase K, recombinant, PCR grade Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3115836001
Rinse once in DEPC PBT.
Post-fix with 4% PFA 1X DEPC PBS for 5 min. 00:05:00
Three 5 min washes in DEPC PBT.

Acetylation, 10 min, room temperature. Add 625 µl acetic anhydride to 250 mL 0.1M TEA (triethanolamine, prepped with DEPC water) and shake well—use immediately. Acetic anhydride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #320102-1L Triethanolamine hydrochloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T1502
Rinse in DEPC PBT.
Wash twice in DEPC PBT, 5 min each.
Rinse in DEPC H 2 O.

Prepare a humidified slide box. To do this, stand a small slide box on its end and roll a cylinder of paper towels that can fit into the bottom of the box (this will take up about a fifth of the slots). Soak the paper towels in ~25mL of 5X SSC/50% formamide. Set this on your bench with the remaining slots ready to accept the slides. a Formamide, deionized American Bio Catalog #AB00600-00500

Take each slide individually and gently let the water wick off onto a Kimwipe. Wipe the edges of the slide with the Kimwipe so that a border of dry slide surrounds the tissue. Continue on to the next step before the tissue sections dry. This step should only take ~1 min/ slide. a
